what_it_does: Wires the RLS for the dedicated least-privilege 'company boundary' principal and seeds the shared design channel. Defines is_company_boundary() (a stable SQL function reading app_metadata.role='company_boundary' from auth.jwt()). Creates INSERT+SELECT policies keyed on that function for channel_posts and the three collab tables (dna_tokens, design_seeds, design_submissions), plus SELECT+UPDATE policies on channels (no create). Seeds the 'design' channel (shared=true), adds 'design' to the claude-design client's channels array, and grants INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on channel_posts and INSERT/UPDATE on the collab/channels tables to authenticated so the boundary RLS policies can actually let writes through.
observations: Policies key off a STABLE custom JWT claim (app_metadata.role) rather than a hardcoded uid, explicitly for local-vs-prod portability.
is_company_boundary() is declared LANGUAGE sql STABLE with coalesce-to-empty-string default.
All policies use drop-if-exists then create for idempotent replay.
The boundary principal gets INSERT+SELECT on the shared slice, SELECT+UPDATE on channels, but explicitly may NOT create channels.
Comment clarifies grants confer privileges while RLS only filters — without the grants the boundary INSERTs 403 despite the policy.
Seeds the 'design' channel with shared=true and members '[]', on conflict do nothing.
Updates clients.channels via array(select distinct unnest(...)) to dedupe-append 'design'.
service_role still bypasses RLS for the Edge Function's audited writes; this narrows only the boundary principal.
standouts: The dedicated least-privilege boundary principal instead of using the service-role master key for company-side writes.
Claim-based (not uid-based) policy design for portability across local/prod — one migration works in both.
The explicit grant-vs-RLS distinction documented in the privileges block (a common 403 footgun called out).
conventions: Completes the RLS story that 0003/0004 scaffolded (they enabled RLS; this writes the scoped policies).
Helper function + policy pattern matching standard Supabase RLS idioms (auth.jwt() claim read).
concerns: Grants INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on channel_posts to ALL authenticated, relying entirely on RLS policies to restrict to the boundary principal — any other authenticated role with a write policy could write; correctness depends on no broad write policy existing elsewhere.
Header references a separate 0006 (design_client policies) that this batch does not include — the full RLS picture is split across files not all seen here.
DELETE granted on channel_posts but no boundary DELETE policy is created in this file, so the grant is currently unusable by the boundary principal (latent).
notes: Completes the security model: defines the claim-based boundary principal, scopes it via RLS to the shared slice (insert+select, channels select+update only), grants the underlying privileges, and seeds the shared 'design' channel + grants it to the claude-design client.
questions: Where is the 0006 design_client policy file that the comments repeatedly reference?
Why grant DELETE on channel_posts when no boundary DELETE policy is created here?
How is the company_boundary app_metadata.role provisioned on the boundary Supabase user?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: stated as the boundary principal RLS + seed channel; the file defines the helper, the scoped policies, the grants, and the seed exactly as described.
apparent_themes: least-privilege dedicated principal over master-key writes
fail-closed scoping narrowed to the shared slice
grants enable, RLS filters (the two-layer access model)
intention_signals: Give the company-side outbound writer exactly the access it needs (write the shared slice, read it back, bump channels) and no more, using a portable JWT claim so the same migration secures both local and prod, then open the shared design channel for use.
novelty: The dedicated company_boundary principal scoped purely by a portable custom claim, and the explicit grant-vs-policy reasoning that makes the boundary writes actually succeed.
contribution: Brings the boundary-principal authorization layer and the seed shared channel — the scoped RLS policies, helper function, privilege grants, and the 'design' channel that activate the shared substrate for least-privilege company-side use.
summary_for_embedding: SQL migration defining the is_company_boundary() claim helper, scoped INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE RLS policies for the boundary principal across channel_posts/collab/channels, privilege grants, and a seeded shared 'design' channel.
intention_for_embedding: To scope a dedicated least-privilege company boundary principal via a portable JWT claim to exactly the shared-slice writes/reads and open the shared design channel for the Claude Design collaboration.
